description_long: This is a rustic elevated cabin constructed primarily from weathered wood and topped with a large, irregularly patched corrugated metal roof in a deep reddish-brown color. The roof features multiple overlapping panels, giving it a makeshift, pieced-together appearance, and extends well beyond the walls, providing ample overhang. The cabin is supported by tall, slender wooden stilts, raising it above the ground, and is accessed by a simple wooden ladder attached to one side. The walls are made of vertical wooden planks, some of which appear uneven or slightly warped, contributing to the structure's aged and utilitarian look. There are open window spaces on the longer sides, with no visible glass, and the overall design suggests a functional shelter suited for rural or informal urban settings.
description: A weathered, stilted wooden cabin with a large, patchwork corrugated metal roof and open window spaces, accessed by a wooden ladder. The structure has a rustic, makeshift appearance with prominent overhanging roof panels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, overhanging corrugated metal roof, open window spaces, and the wooden ladder leading up to the cabin.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sharply peaked roof, the vertical wooden plank wall, and the ladder attached to the left side.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the overhanging roof and open window spaces visible, but the ladder is less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full height of the stilts, the peaked roof, and a solid wooden wall with no windows or doors.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with irregular, overlapping corrugated metal panels and deep overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple medium-sized rectangular open windows asymmetrically distributed on the longer sides, with no glass or shutters visible
attribute_door_placement: no exterior door is visible in any view; access appears to be via the open window space and ladder on the front side
